sources.publisher: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.quote: Quitlines provide free coaching over the phone to help you quit smoking ... FDA-approved medications include nicotine replacement products (patch, lozenge, gum, nasal spray, oral inhaler), varenicline and bupropion SR ... Preparation is key to quitting successfully, and making a quit plan is the first step.
Tobacco use is the most common preventable cause of death ... a mixture of counseling and quit-smoking medicine has been shown to be the best method.

factsExtracted.value: Quitting smoking addresses nicotine addiction and its health consequences. Recommended approaches include counseling, FDA-approved nicotine replacement products (patch, lozenge, gum, nasal spray, oral inhaler), prescription medications (varenicline and bupropion SR), and behavioral support such as creating a quit plan and using quitlines.
Reducing risk for heart disease, cancer, lung disease, COPD, diabetes, stroke, and pregnancy complications, as documented in CDC condition-specific resources
Tobacco use, identified by MedlinePlus as the most common preventable cause of death
U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
